summary: Found 3 new issues not already tracked in BUGS.md: one P2 around bypassing PartnerShell context, plus two P3 UX/accessibility problems in the nested shell layout and async error state.

findings.title: Profile ignores PartnerShell context and re-fetches user/partner data
findings.description: `ProfileContent` does its own `auth.me()`/`Partner.get()` load even though it is always rendered inside `PartnerShell`, which already resolves `currentUser`, `userRole`, and the effective `partner` (including StaffAccessLink-based access). For director/managing_director users whose `User.partner` is null, this page can incorrectly fall back to "no restaurant" and show a mismatched role, and every visit pays for a second blocking load/error path after the shell has already loaded.
findings.suggested_fix: Consume `usePartnerAccess()` inside `ProfileContent` for `currentUser`, `userRole`, and `partner`, seed local editable state from that context, and keep only the `updateMe()` mutation local.

findings.title: Nested `min-h-screen` containers create extra viewport height inside PartnerShell
findings.description: The ready state uses `min-h-screen`, and the loading/error branches do the same. Because `PartnerShell` already wraps children inside its own full-height layout with header and nav above `<main>`, this adds another 100vh inside the content area. On short/mobile screens that produces unnecessary scroll space and leaves the spinner/error content visually off-center.
findings.suggested_fix: Remove inner `min-h-screen` usage for content rendered under `PartnerShell`; size the page to the shell content area with `w-full`, `h-full`, or a smaller local min-height instead.

findings.title: Load error state is not announced when async fetch fails
findings.description: When `auth.me()` fails, the component replaces the live loading region with a static error message and button, but the error container is not an alert and focus is not moved. In an SPA flow, screen-reader users may never be told that loading finished with an error.
findings.suggested_fix: Give the error message/container `role="alert"` (or otherwise move focus to the error heading/button) when `isLoadError` becomes true.

missing_tests: Profile under `PartnerShell` with a StaffAccessLink-only user (`User.partner = null`) should display the shell-resolved restaurant/role without a second full-page loader.
Profile rendered inside `PartnerShell` should not add extra viewport-height scroll space in loading, error, or ready states on mobile-sized viewports.
An accessibility test should verify that the async load failure is announced to assistive tech via `role="alert"` or focus transfer.
notes: Did not run the app; findings are from static review only.

Did not repeat already-tracked issues from BUGS.md such as missing `activeTab`, missing 44px inputs, inline save-footer regression, partner-load error handling, decorative icons, or the null role-label fallback.
